intended_use: ClariPulmo is a non-invasive image analysis software for use with CT images which is intended to support the quantification of lung CT images. The software is designed to support the physician in the
intended_use_full: ClariPulmo is a non-invasive image analysis software for use with CT images which is intended to support the quantification of lung CT images. The software is designed to support the physician in the diagnosis and documentation of pulmonary tissue images (e.g., abnormalities) from the CT thoracic datasets. (The software is not intended for the diagnosis of pneumonia or COVID-19). The software provides automated segmentation of the lungs and quantification of low-attenuation and high-attenuation areas within the segmented lungs by using predefined Hounsfield unit thresholds.
target_condition: Pulmonary tissue abnormalities detectable on CT thoracic imaging
ground_truth: Expert-established segmentations (manual vs. threshold-based segmentations) and expert radiologist's imageJ-based segmentation
other_metrics: HAA analysis: Pearson correlation coefficient (PCC) 0.980–0.983 with expert-established segmentations; LAA analysis: PCC 0.99 with expert-established segmentations; AI-based lung segmentation: PCC 0.977–0.992 and DICE coefficients 0.98–0.99 with statistical significance across normal/LAA/HAA patients, CT scanner, reconstructed kernel, and low-dose subgroups
